course_description:
This industry placement course will be taken in the final year of the Bachelor of Occupational Health and Safety Science (Honours). This course will provide real industry experience in an approved workplace.

Students will be expected to be present in the workplace during normal working hours and undertake OHS related work as agreed between the workplace supervisor and the placement coordinator. Over the duration of the placement it is anticipated that the student will be exposed to a range of OHS professional competencies and will reflect upon these in a work journal.

For more information about enrolling in this course, visit science.uq.edu.au/placements.

Final year BOHSSc students will be required to undertake 240 hours of work within a nominated workplace, under the guidance of both a workplace supervisor and an academic supervisor.ﾠThis work integrated learning experience provides an opportunity for students to apply the OHS knowledge, skills, and capabilities gained during their university studies within the workplace.

prerequisites: You'll need to complete the following courses before enrolling in this one:
restrictions: Restricted to BOHSSc (Hons) with permission to enrol. See course description for further information.

timetable:
Additional timetable information
This course involves 240 hours of industry work at a host organisation which equates to two days a week for 15ﾠweeks (24ﾠFebruary 2025 to 6 June 2025).ﾠ Where students are completing OHSS4012 and OHSS4014 concurrently with a host organisation, students are expected to attend the host organisation four days per week for 15ﾠweeks (24ﾠFebruary 2025ﾠto 6 June 2025).
course_aims: This course aims to provide students with an opportunity to integrate OHS theoretical knowledge and skills by undertaking an industry placement with an approved host organisation.

learning_outcomes.description: Integrate and apply OHS knowledge to the work situation.
Acquire practical skills that will enhance the application of existing knowledge.
Plan strategies to address and solve OHS existing or emerging issues.
Show appropriate levels of competence in the application of OHS and related skills and practice.
Demonstrate the ability to effectively communicate orally and in writing with both workers and management.
Appraise their own performance by reflective practice.

assessment_details.title: Learning Contract
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students are to develop a learning contract in consultation with their workplace supervisor that outlines the six OHS tasks the student will undertake during the placement. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets required.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the Additional assessment information section further below for information relating to extension and deferral applications.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.title: OHS Tasks
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Students are required to undertake a range of OHS tasks that demonstrate their ability to apply OHS theoretical knowledge and technical/non-technical skills in practice. During the 240 hour placement, students are to complete OHS tasks and submit six (6) of these tasks during the semester. For each task, students are required to complete the OHS Task Form and upload supporting evidence to Turnitin. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task (subject to host supervisor's approval). Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets required.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
See the Additional assessment information section further below for information relating to extension and deferral applications.
Late submission
This assessment is graded on a pass/fail basis, and there is no system for applying "mark" penalties. If the assessment item is not submitted by the submission due date outlined in the course profile, it will be considered a non-submission, resulting in a "fail".

learning_activities.topic: UQ Orientation to OHS Placements This session will cover course expectations, requirements and assessments. Learning outcomes: L03, L06

Host Organisation Orientation Students will spend Week 1 orientating themselves to their workplace, including the undertaking of compulsory induction programs and developing a learning contract. Learning outcomes: L03, L05

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 2 To Week 15 (03 Mar - 08 Jun)
learning_activities.topic: OHS Activity Students will spend weeks 2 - 15 completing OHS tasks as outlined in their learning contract. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life.
